turns.en: If you want to see a vision of the future, the top 20 and even the top 100 is totally dominated by China. It's China and a little bit of Korea and Taiwan. Are you in the top 20 in the world? Top 20 in DIA?
turns.zh: 如果你想看看未来的图景，前 20 名甚至前 100 名完全被中国主导。是中国，外加一点韩国和台湾。你在世界前 20 吗？DIA 前 20？

turns.en: Yeah, yeah. Do you want to tell everybody your handle? No, no, don't tell them. It's not worth it. Well, they actually listed me with my actual name in the list. Oh, did they really? Interesting. But yeah, there's only two Americans in the top 20. The rest, almost everyone, is from Asia.
turns.zh: 是的，是的。你想告诉大家你的账号吗？不，不，别告诉他们。不值得。不过他们确实在名单上用了我的真名。哦，真的吗？有意思。但确实，前 20 里只有两个美国人，其余几乎都来自亚洲。

turns.en: We were talking about something I think is really important. People always think video games are frivolous, but what you were saying is that it's so difficult it requires you to only think about that, and it can relieve stress, take out the rest of the world because it's so hard.
turns.zh: 我们之前聊到一个我认为非常重要的话题。人们总认为电子游戏是 frivolous 的，但你说的是，它太难了，迫使你只能想着它，从而缓解压力，把世界其他部分隔绝在外。

turns.en: Yeah, you can only think about that. I mean, if I play a video game on Extreme difficulty, then I have to concentrate fully on the game, and it has a calming effect. It sort of chills down. And I think many people, if you play martial arts or pool, something that forces you to concentrate fully, actually has a calming effect. I find it has a kind of mental restoring effect. Jiu-Jitsu is like that, archery is like that as well. When you're shooting a bow, you have to think only of it, and it cleans the mind.
turns.zh: 是的，你只能想着它。我的意思是，如果我玩最高难度的电子游戏，我就必须全神贯注，这反而有镇静效果。它会让人冷静下来。而且我觉得很多人，如果你练武术或打台球，任何迫使你全神贯注的事情，其实都有镇静效果。我发现它有某种精神恢复作用。柔术就是这样，射箭也是。当你拉弓时，你只能想着它，这能清理思绪。

ts.sec: Study on surgeons who play video games

turns.en: I was reading this study about surgeons where they found that surgeons who regularly play video games make fewer errors. It makes sense because video games require manual dexterity. Look at this: 32% fewer errors, 24% faster, and scored 26% better overall than their non-player colleagues.
turns.zh: 我读过一项关于外科医生的研究，发现经常玩电子游戏的外科医生犯的错误更少。这说得通，因为电子游戏需要手部灵活性。你看：比不玩游戏的同事少 32% 的错误，快 24%，整体表现好 26%。

turns.en: Oh, I believe that for sure. That's incredible. You should be required in medical school to play video games. If somebody is a top-ranked video game player and they say they're a surgeon, I'd be like plus one, plus two. Top-ranked for sure. But this isn't even top-ranked, this is just people who play. Your manual dexterity has to be extremely high. You're looking at things on the screen, reacting, and you have like 10 milliseconds to react. So if somebody has incredible reaction times and manual dexterity, they're obviously going to be a good surgeon. Imagine if there was a course that would make you 26% better. Everyone would have to take that course. Why would you want a surgeon that's less prepared? You'd say, 'Hey Bob, did you take this course? You didn't? Don't you understand this course makes you 26% better?' Sure, you would have to take it. Everyone should have to play video games if you want to be a surgeon. Well, I think it would be a very good test. If somebody can't play video games well, that means... because you have to move both hands simultaneously, react very fast to things on the screen. And if your keystrokes or mouse clicks are wrong, you lose the game. So if somebody has a good rank in video games, I would say their manual dexterity must be extremely good.
turns.zh: 哦，我完全相信。这太不可思议了。医学院应该要求玩电子游戏。如果有人是顶级玩家，又说自己是外科医生，我会觉得加分，加两分。顶级玩家肯定。但这甚至不是顶级，只是玩的人。你的手部灵活性必须极高。你看着屏幕上的东西，做出反应，只有大约 10 毫秒的反应时间。所以如果有人反应速度和手部灵活性惊人，他们显然会成为好外科医生。想象一下，如果有一门课程能让你好 26%，每个人都会去上。为什么你想要一个准备不足的外科医生？你会说，‘嘿 Bob，你上过这门课吗？没有？你不知道这门课能让你好 26% 吗？’当然，你必须上。如果你想当外科医生，每个人都应该玩电子游戏。嗯，我认为这会是一个很好的测试。如果有人玩不好电子游戏，那意味着……因为你必须同时移动双手，对屏幕上的东西快速反应。如果你的按键或鼠标点击错了，你就输了。所以如果某人在电子游戏中排名很高，我会说他们的手部灵活性一定非常好。

ts.sec: Motor skills and exceptional human performance

turns.en: Motor skills have to be excellent. If you think about Starcraft or Quake, any game where a lot of people are playing to rise to the top, you have to be exceptional as a human being. There has to be something exceptional about you.
turns.zh: 运动技能必须出色。想想《星际争霸》或《雷神之锤》，任何很多人玩并力争上游的游戏，你必须作为人类出类拔萃。你身上必须有某种非凡之处。

turns.en: Yeah. Actually, Quake way back in the day, I was one of the world's best Quake players. I know we talked about this. I loved Quake. My final semester in college, I probably put more time into Quake than all my college classes.
turns.zh: 是的。实际上，很久以前玩《雷神之锤》时，我是世界上最好的玩家之一。我知道我们聊过这个。我爱《雷神之锤》。大学最后一个学期，我花在《雷神之锤》上的时间可能比所有课程都多。

turns.en: When I was on NewsRadio, all the writers were super nerds. They had a LAN set up at the studio where they all played Quake. I had never played video games, and I would go in to hang out with them. We'd get silly and start playing Quake against each other. I got addicted hardcore. I got a T1 line installed in my house. I went hardcore. You're checking how many milliseconds of latency. I was fully addicted. I was making my own computers, going to Fry's Hardware, buying motherboards, putting everything together. But it was too much of a time suck. I'm an obsessive person. I can't get involved in golf. It's too slow. I know a lot of people find golf good, and if you think of it as walking outdoors with friends and occasionally hitting a ball, that's cool. It does require concentration when hitting the ball, but it's too slow for me. Nothing compares to video games in terms of the amount of feedback, the sensory overload. You have a large high-resolution screen, a fast computer, headphones with sounds from all directions, rockets flying by. There's nothing like that.
turns.zh: 我在《新闻广播》工作时，所有编剧都是超级极客。他们在演播室搭了局域网，一起玩《雷神之锤》。我从来没玩过电子游戏，但会去和他们混在一起。我们变得傻乎乎的，开始互相玩《雷神之锤》。我上瘾了，重度上瘾。我在家里装了 T1 线路。我彻底沉迷了。你会检查多少毫秒的延迟。我完全上瘾了。我自己组装电脑，去 Fry's 硬件店买主板，把所有东西拼起来。但这太耗时间了。我是个容易沉迷的人。我不能碰高尔夫，太慢了。我知道很多人觉得高尔夫不错，如果你把它看作和朋友户外散步，偶尔击球，那很酷。击球时确实需要专注，但对我来说太慢了。没有什么比得上电子游戏的反馈量和感官过载。你有一个大尺寸高分辨率屏幕，一台快速电脑，耳机里传来四面八方的声音，火箭从身边飞过。没有别的能比。

ts.sec: Golf addiction vs video game intensity

turns.en: But I think golf still is... Jamie will tell you, he's a Golf Nut. It's super addictive and takes like eight hours a day. Once you get into golf, I guess any sport gets super addictive. But for me, the intensity of video games is hard to beat. And people dismiss it because they think it's just a waste of time, but we're showing real-world benefits. If you want to be a drone operator, it's the only game in town. Being really good at video games helps.
turns.zh: 但我认为高尔夫仍然……Jamie 会告诉你，他是个高尔夫狂。它超级上瘾，每天要花八个小时。一旦你迷上高尔夫，我想任何运动都会变得超级上瘾。但对我来说，电子游戏的强度难以超越。人们 dismiss 它，因为他们认为这只是浪费时间，但我们展示了现实世界的好处。如果你想当无人机操作员，这是唯一的选择。擅长电子游戏很有帮助。

turns.en: Yeah, for sure. In fact, I can tell what my mental acuity is if I play a very hard video game. If I'm trying to get an extremely good clear time in Diablo or something, or any first-person shooter, I can tell that I'm tired or my brain's not working as well as it should. It's like a mental...
turns.zh: 是的，当然。事实上，如果我玩一个很难的电子游戏，我能判断自己的 mental acuity。如果我想在《暗黑破坏神》或任何第一人称射击游戏中取得极好的通关时间，我能感觉到自己累了，或者大脑运转不如平时。这就像一种 mental……

turns.en: Calibration you can tell immediately like what is what, how good is your mental state. If you're trying to play really well, if you play late at night and you're tired, you would just play badly. You can say okay, you may think that your brain is working well but it isn't because you play the video game and you're like, you suck. So you're putting it under stress, you're really stress testing it. Sometimes like oh I think I'm fine, but then you play the game like okay I'm not, I might be 10% below what I should be.
turns.zh: 校准能立刻告诉你——你的精神状态怎么样。如果你想打得特别好，深夜玩又累，就会打得很烂。你会觉得大脑状态不错，但其实不是，因为一打游戏就发现自己很菜。所以你在给它施压，真正在压力测试它。有时候我觉得自己状态挺好，但一打游戏就发现不对，可能比正常水平低了 10%。

turns.en: That's how I feel about workouts for sure. That's how I knew I had COVID. I knew everyone in my family had COVID and I was trying not to get it, so I was working out. I was like something's up, I felt fine normally, but then during exercise I was like okay I can tell there's something wrong here, so let's back off, relax.
turns.zh: 我健身时就是这种感觉。我就是这么发现自己得了新冠的。我知道家里人都得了，自己一直想躲开，所以去健身。感觉有点不对劲，平时觉得挺好，但一运动就觉得有问题，于是赶紧停下来休息。

turns.en: People who don't stress test their mind think they're operating on the same level all the time. Sometimes I come in here and I can't form a sentence, and I don't know what it is. It's like what is going on? Sleep wasn't that good or something like that. I'm too busy and the words aren't coming out. I know how to talk, I talk professionally, and I can't talk. Sleep is massive, huge. So if I want to tell if I got a good night's sleep or not, I just play a video game for five minutes. I'm like okay my sleep wasn't that good. Sometimes your brain will recover through the day, an hour or two after waking up it's better, because your brain does kind of recover from a bad night's sleep a little bit.
turns.zh: 那些不给大脑做压力测试的人，总以为自己状态一直在线。有时候我走进来，连句子都说不完整，不知道怎么回事。就是不对劲。可能是睡眠不好，或者太忙了，话就是说不出来。我明明会说话，还是专业讲话的，可就是说不出来。睡眠太重要了，太关键了。所以我想知道自己睡得好不好，就打五分钟游戏。一打就知道没睡好。有时候白天大脑会恢复一些，醒来一两个小时之后会好一点，因为大脑确实能从糟糕的睡眠中稍微恢复过来。

turns.en: Creatine apparently does help. Creatine is actually a nootropic, believe it or not. There are a lot of benefits of creatine that are really weird. Are there any downsides?
turns.zh: 肌酸好像确实有帮助。肌酸其实是一种益智药，信不信由你。它有很多很神奇的好处。有副作用吗？

turns.en: No, it's natural part of food. Especially for women, apparently especially postmenopausal women, it's very beneficial. There are a lot of cognitive benefits. One of the big ones they found recently is performance when sleep deprived. Mental performance when sleep deprived increases pretty measurably when you supplement with creatine.
turns.zh: 没有，它是食物中的天然成分。尤其对女性，特别是绝经后女性，非常有益。有很多认知方面的好处。最近发现的一大好处是睡眠剥夺时的表现。补充肌酸后，睡眠剥夺时的脑力表现会有明显提升。

turns.en: Is creatine naturally occurring in steak?
turns.zh: 肌酸是牛排里天然存在的吗？

turns.en: Yeah, it's naturally occurring in meat. I think it's primarily an animal-based thing. I switched to steak and eggs for breakfast and I found that's like a power-up.
turns.zh: 对，肉里天然就有。我觉得主要是动物性的。我早餐改吃牛排和鸡蛋，发现就像吃了能量包一样。

turns.en: We're all overrun with carbohydrates. Carbohydrates make this big crash, the rise and the crash. You stay flat if you eat a primarily high protein, high fat diet. Your body runs off ketosis essentially.
turns.zh: 我们都被碳水化合物淹没了。碳水化合物会让你大起大落，升上去又跌下来。如果你主要吃高蛋白高脂肪的饮食，血糖就会很平稳。身体基本上靠生酮供能。

turns.en: I just have steak and eggs, no bread or anything, and it's great actually. It's a power-up. I'd say people dismiss this whole carnivore diet thing because in our heads there's a lot of propaganda that animal agriculture is the number one contributor to global warming. This rubbish, not true. It does not matter. Not only is it not, but the real problem is factory farming. Regenerative farming is carbon neutral if not sequesters carbon. The animals are not going to make any difference to global warming, like none, zero, nothing.
turns.zh: 我就吃牛排和鸡蛋，不吃面包之类的，感觉特别好，就像能量包。我觉得人们之所以否定全肉饮食，是因为我们脑子里有很多宣传，说畜牧业是全球变暖的头号元凶。这是胡说，不是真的。根本没关系。不仅没关系，真正的问题是工厂化养殖。再生农业如果不是碳汇，至少也是碳中和。动物对全球变暖不会有任何影响，一点都没有，零。

turns.en: Do you think that's just propaganda because of people that have a vested interest in plant-based meat products and green energy?
turns.zh: 你觉得这只是因为那些在植物肉和绿色能源上有既得利益的人在宣传吗？

turns.en: I think it's part of it. You're generally going to get people pushing to avoid meat. Some people maybe have a financial interest, maybe they're just vegetarians or vegans for ideological reasons. But it's not going to make any difference to global warming or the CO2 concentration in the atmosphere really, if people eat fewer steaks. It doesn't matter, it's irrelevant. I want to be super clear about that. It will not matter. You will not even be able to measure it. That's how irrelevant it is.
turns.zh: 我觉得这是部分原因。总会有人劝你少吃肉。有些人可能有经济利益，有些人只是出于意识形态原因吃素或纯素。但就算人们少吃牛排，对全球变暖或大气二氧化碳浓度也不会有任何影响。无关紧要，我想说清楚这一点。不会有影响，你甚至都测不出来。就是这么无关紧要。

turns.en: Isn't it funny that that's unmeasurable? Heretic speaking, that's crazy talk nowadays. It's like you have to say that we have to eat less meat, that meat is bad. Eat as much as you want, it's not going to make a difference. Sing it to the world. If somebody says it does make a difference, I'm like how will you measure it? If you can't even measure it, then it's...
turns.zh: 这都测不出来，是不是很好笑？异端言论，现在这简直是疯话。好像你必须说少吃肉、肉不好。想吃多少就吃多少，根本不会有影响。跟全世界说去。如果有人非说会有影响，我就问你怎么测？如果根本测不出来，那不就是……

turns.en: Literally you won't be able to measure it.
turns.zh: 真的，你根本测不出来。

turns.en: There's so much out today, first of all all directions. Thank you so much for buying Twitter. Thank you so much. I'm not exaggerating when I think you changed the course of history. I really do. I really think you made a fork in the road. We were headed down a path of censorship and of control of narratives that is unprecedented. Forget about what they were able to do back when they had newspapers and the media under control. What they were doing with social media by suppressing information, and when you had a combined government effort like with what they were doing with the laptop story, you have 51 former intelligence agents saying that this is Russian disinformation, take it off offline, and Twitter complied. If you didn't buy that, we wouldn't have known that. We had no idea.
turns.zh: 今天外面发生了太多事，首先四面八方。非常感谢你买下 Twitter，真的非常感谢。我觉得你改变了历史进程，一点不夸张。我真的这么认为。你真的让历史走到了一个岔路口。我们原本正走向一条前所未有的审查和叙事控制之路。别说什么他们当年控制报纸和媒体的时候了。他们在社交媒体上压制信息，再加上政府联手，比如笔记本故事那件事，51 名前情报人员说这是俄罗斯的虚假信息，要求下线，Twitter 就照做了。如果你没买下它，我们根本不会知道这些。我们完全不知道。

turns.en: The reason I bought it was because I'm pretty attuned. Since I was the most interacted with user on Twitter before the acquisition. Before the acquisition, I had more interactions than some accounts like Obama and whatever had higher follower counts, but I had the most number of interactions of any account in the system. So I was very attuned to if they changed the system, I could tell immediately. I'm like something weird is going on here. I just got increasingly uneasy. Obviously when they deplatformed the sitting president, deplatformed Trump, that was just insane. The things he was posting, he was posting good things. He was saying hey we don't riot, don't destroy property, please stay calm. That's the kind of stuff he was posting. And you're like what's wrong with that? Then some people say oh that's like some sort of dog whistle, he means the opposite. I'm like okay, so we'll give you Trump's account now, you post what you think he should post, because he can post nothing.
turns.zh: 我买它的原因是我很敏感。因为收购前我是 Twitter 上互动最多的用户。收购前，我的互动次数比奥巴马等粉丝更多的账号还要多，我是系统里互动最多的账号。所以如果系统有变化，我能立刻察觉。我就觉得有点不对劲。越来越不安。显然，当他们把在任总统禁言，把特朗普禁言，那简直疯了。他发的东西，他发的是好东西。他说我们不要暴乱，不要破坏财产，请保持冷静。他发的就是这些。然后你问这有什么问题？有些人说那是某种狗哨，他意思相反。我说好吧，那我们把特朗普的账号给你，你来发你觉得他该发的东西，因为他什么都不能发了。

ts.sec: On political violence and agent provocateurs

turns.en: Can ask people to calm down like what it was insane like it didn't make any sense.
turns.zh: 可以要求人们冷静下来，就像那太疯狂了，根本说不通。

turns.en: Well, it's completely a local when you say it's dog whistling to tell his followers to not be violent. That's crazy. Crazy. That's crazy. Don't you think they'll listen to him? Yeah, isn't that the whole point? They listened to him and created violence in the first place. That's what you think. That's what you're accusing him of, right? And then there's the fact that we know that there were agents in the crowd that were agent provocateurs that were encouraging people to do illegal. We know that for a fact. This is not that was always the big Alex Jones type 10 hat conspiracy theory, because Alex proposed that back at the World Trade Organization protests, I believe we in Seattle in the 90s, and they sent in agent provocateurs, started smashing things, lighting things on fire. Now all of a sudden a peaceful protest is no longer peaceful. They move in the cops, they shut everything down. They had it set up where it was a no protest zone where you couldn't even have a pin that had the WTO with a red line through it. They wouldn't let you go in through to go to work, so you couldn't protest, you couldn't exercise your First Amendment rights. You couldn't even have a peaceful protest, a sticker on your car. You couldn't have that. It's crazy. It is crazy.
turns.zh: 嗯，这完全是地方性的。你说这是用狗哨告诉他的追随者不要暴力，那太疯狂了。疯狂。太疯狂了。你不觉得他们会听他的吗？是啊，这不就是重点吗？他们听了他的话，首先制造了暴力。那是你的想法。你是在指控他，对吧？而且还有一个事实，我们知道人群中有特工，是煽动者，在鼓励人们做违法的事。我们确凿地知道这一点。这并非总是那种 Alex Jones 式的 10 美元帽子阴谋论，因为 Alex 在世界贸易组织抗议活动时就提出过这个，我相信是 90 年代在西雅图。他们派了煽动者，开始打砸东西、放火。突然间，和平抗议不再和平了。他们出动警察，关闭了一切。他们设置了一个禁止抗议的区域，你甚至不能戴一个带有 WTO 标志并画着红线的徽章。他们不让你进去上班，所以你无法抗议，无法行使你的第一修正案权利。你甚至不能进行和平抗议，不能在车上贴贴纸。你不能那样做。这太疯狂了。确实疯狂。

turns.en: So no, I think we're very much at a fork in the road in destiny, and you know, so I mean the reason I did the Twitter acquisition was like, man, if I don't do this, I think we're screwed. Well, you didn't do it, no one else was going to do it because it wasn't a financial winner. It was kind of a crazy move. It's a crazy move. I mean, the thing was way overpriced, and you know, long term I think we can ultimately make it a win for investors, but boy, this is a hard way to make a living.
turns.zh: 所以不，我认为我们正处在一个命运的十字路口，你知道，我收购 Twitter 的原因就是，伙计，如果我不这么做，我觉得我们就完蛋了。嗯，你没有做，没有其他人会做，因为这不是一笔财务上的赢家。这有点疯狂。这是一个疯狂的行动。我的意思是，这东西定价过高，而且你知道，长期来看我认为我们最终可以为投资者带来胜利，但天哪，这是一种艰难的谋生方式。

It wasn't worth $44 billion, you morons. Wrong. And also you're not taking into account the advertiser boycott. Exactly. So there are these organizations, like you can tell, they have an Orwellian name, like the Center for Countering Digital Hate, which is a total scam organization, because they're like the Ministry of Truth type of thing in 1984. turns.zh: 嗯，我们过去和现在都面临一场大规模的广告商抵制，由一群左翼非政府组织策划，你知道，而且你总是想——我本该带上我的帽子，我有一顶帽子写着“让所有虚构再次伟大”。我见过那顶帽子。是的，我本该带上我的“让石油虚构”帽子。但是，嗯，我的意思是这完全疯了。所以如果你不做，没人会做。而且我一直从白痴那里听到一个可笑的说法：“埃隆是个糟糕的商人，Twitter 的价值比他购买时低了 400%。”不，它一开始就不值那个价。它不值 440 亿美元，你们这些白痴。错了。而且你还没有考虑到广告商抵制。正是这样。所以有一些组织，你可以看出来，它们有奥威尔式的名字，比如“打击数字仇恨中心”，那完全是一个诈骗组织，因为它们就像《1984》里的真理部。你知道，它们是一个审查组织。它们推动广告商抵制。所以我们仍然有一些抵制正在开始解除，我认为如果特朗普赢了，我们会看到大部分抵制解除。但如果卡玛拉赢了，我们会看到抵制变得更强烈，它们会发疯——它们会关闭。卡玛拉政权不可能允许 X 存在。

turns.en: Yes. What would they do? Well, I mean they can just sic the DOJ on you, you know, and say like, they've had this whole thing about hate speech, misinformation, whatever. Except they're the ones pushing the misinformation. But that doesn't stop them from filing massive lawsuits and using the DOJ. I mean, the DOJ has been attacking SpaceX, for example, for not hiring asylum seekers, even though it is legal for SpaceX to hire anyone who is not a permanent resident of the US. So we're damned if we do and damned if we don't. There's an example of what DOJ can do. So it's illegal to hire someone who's not an American citizen for what SpaceX is considered an advanced weapons technology. So it's covered by International Traffic in Arms Regulations because we make rocket technology that can be used against the United States. So like if North Korea or Iran got SpaceX rocket technology, they could use that to launch nukes at America. That would be bad. Yeah, that'd be really bad. So since we are in the most extreme category of weapons technology at SpaceX, under US ITAR law, it is illegal for us to hire anyone who's not a permanent resident, because the presumption is that if they're not a permanent resident, they're going to return to their home country and take the rocket technology with them. So it's illegal for us to hire anyone who either has not—they can have a green card or be a citizen, they just have to be a permanent resident of the United States. Then there's another law that says if you discriminate against asylum seekers, that's also breaking the law. So they just—the DOJ can only do a small number of big lawsuits every year. They launched a giant lawsuit against SpaceX saying that SpaceX discriminated against asylum seekers. And we're like, but it's illegal for us to hire anyone who's not a permanent resident. So we're in this—this is what I mean, it's like the Orwellian situation is getting insane. You're damned if you do and you're damned if you don't. So you're damned.
turns.zh: 是的。他们会怎么做？嗯，我的意思是他们可以派司法部来找你，你知道，然后说，他们一直在搞什么仇恨言论、虚假信息之类的。但正是他们在传播虚假信息。但这并不能阻止他们提起大规模诉讼并利用司法部。我的意思是，司法部一直在攻击 SpaceX，例如，因为不雇佣寻求庇护者，尽管 SpaceX 雇佣任何不是美国永久居民的人是合法的。所以我们进退两难。这是一个司法部能做什么的例子。所以对于 SpaceX 来说，雇佣不是美国公民的人是非法的，因为 SpaceX 被认为是先进武器技术。所以它受《国际武器贸易条例》管辖，因为我们制造可以用来对付美国的火箭技术。所以如果朝鲜或伊朗获得了 SpaceX 的火箭技术，他们可以用它向美国发射核弹。那会很糟糕。是的，那会非常糟糕。所以由于我们在 SpaceX 处于最极端的武器技术类别，根据美国 ITAR 法律，我们雇佣任何不是永久居民的人是非法的，因为假设是如果他们不是永久居民，他们会返回祖国并带走火箭技术。所以我们雇佣任何没有——他们可以持有绿卡或是公民，他们只需要是美国永久居民。然后还有另一条法律规定，如果你歧视寻求庇护者，那也是违法的。所以他们只是——司法部每年只能提起少量的大诉讼。他们对 SpaceX 提起了一项大诉讼，声称 SpaceX 歧视了寻求庇护者。我们想说，但雇佣任何不是永久居民的人是非法的。所以我们处于这种——这就是我的意思，奥威尔式的情况变得疯狂。你进退两难。所以你被定罪了。

turns.en: Can you imagine history looking back, man, at when you watch the robot arms catch the rocket and you realize this is like one of the greatest accomplishments in the history of aerospace. It is one of the wildest accomplishments. When you watch that thing come and you see all the people cheering and it catches it perfectly, like holy—imagine how history is going to look back at the DOJ going after that company. Yeah, how insane it is. A big lawsuit with an army of lawyers. This was not like some minor thing. But it doesn't even make any sense logically. Like how can it even get brought to court if it's illegal?

turns.en: Exactly. So that's what I mean. Like basically if the government wants to go after you, they'll just find a reason. It's like that famous quote from Beria, you know, like Stalin's chief torturer, the head of Stalin's secret police. He's a chief torturer, truly evil human being. This guy Beria. turns.en: Yeah, I mean the sheer number of hoaxes that the Democratic party is pushing over and over again. And it's like, look, I understand politicians are going to exaggerate, they're going to misspeak, and they'll tell occasional untruths, whatever, that's how it is in politics. That's cruel and lacking in empathy.
